name: Accuracy
description: How close your measurement is to the true/correct value

content.overview: Accuracy = how close your measurement is to the true/correct value. Like throwing darts at a bullseye. If all your darts hit the center, you're accurate! {{viz:diagram-01}}
keyPoints: Accuracy = how close to the TRUE value
The thermometer at 99.8°C is more accurate than one at 115°C
You MUST know the true value to judge accuracy
Different from precision (which is about exactness)
content.whyItMatters: In science, accurate measurements matter. A wrong answer is worse than a rough answer. If you're off, your whole conclusion is wrong.
